exemplar.title: To what extent did the economic recession affect the wellbeing of Japanese citizens in terms of depression, anxiety and suicide rates?

detailedExemplarData.title: To what extent did the economic recession affect the wellbeing of Japanese citizens in terms of depression, anxiety and suicide rates?

strengths: Title and introduction clearly communicate the focus and importance of the investigation
Research question is concise, sharply focused, and consistently signposted throughout
Background context (global and Japanese) is comprehensive and directly linked to the studyâs rationale
weaknesses: Methodology is implicit rather than explicitly outlined (criteria for source selection not stated)
Minor drift into adjacent topics (fertility, education) slightly broadens the focus
Historical economic details occasionally lack clear linkage to the mental-health indicators
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Strand assessments indicate consistently strong communication and a sharply focused research question, meriting the upper band. However, implicit methodology and occasional breadth creep prevent a full 6/6, placing the criterion at 5/6.

strengths: Consistent integration of government statistics and scholarly literature underpin most claims
Clear and coherent exposition of IR theories (Gramsciâs hegemony, neoliberalism)
Accurate use of key terminology (karÅshi, neoliberalism) in context
weaknesses: Some citations are incomplete or rely on journalistic sources
Graphs and tables occasionally lack clear labels and provenance
Anecdotal assertions and unsupported claims (e.g., âimperialist adaptationâ) require stronger evidence or revision
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The work shows mostly relevant and effective use of source material and coherent disciplinary knowledge, aligning with the 5â6 band descriptors. Minor lapses in citation precision and occasional generalisations limit the criterion to 4/6.

strengths: Cause-and-effect links between recession, unemployment, and suicide are clearly established
Application of multiple theoretical frameworks demonstrates good analytical engagement
Argument incorporates counter-points (OECD comparison) and evaluates policy responses to some extent
weaknesses: Numerical analysis is thin (no regression or statistical metrics)
Evaluation of counter-arguments is brief and sometimes descriptive
Connection between some indicators (fertility, education) and mental health is asserted rather than demonstrated
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Research quality, analysis, and evaluation each sit in the 4â6 band, reflecting good but not fully critical treatment. The absence of robust quantitative analysis and uneven evaluation justify a holistic score at the lower end of this band (4/12).

strengths: Logical structure with numbered headings and a table of contents supports readability
Consistent layout: labeled tables/figures, Harvard bibliography, title page with word count
Clear definitions of key Japanese terms enhance audience comprehension
weaknesses: Mis-numbered headings in the table of contents interrupt flow
Inconsistent superscript formatting and occasional missing page numbers
Dense paragraphs could be broken into shorter units to improve readability
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Structure and layout meet most upper-band descriptors but feature minor lapses (mis-numbering, formatting inconsistencies). These issues limit the criterion to 3/4.

strandSelections.reasoning: Cause-and-effect links between recession, unemployment and suicide are established and supported by data, and theoretical frameworks (Marxism, neoliberalism, Han) are applied.  However, numerical analysis is thin (no regression or comparative metrics), and some leaps (e.g., linking fertility collapse directly to burnout) are asserted rather than demonstrated.  This reflects adequate-to-good analysis at the lower end of the 4-6 band.

The bibliography lists more than 25 varied sources, including peer-reviewed studies, think-tank reports and official statistics, most of which are pertinent to the RQ.  Some reliance on popular websites and repeated citations of the same document limit evidence of âexcellentâ research, so a solid mid-band 5 is appropriate.

The text follows a logical structure (TOC, numbered headings, sub-sections) that generally supports comprehension.  Some headings are mis-numbered and images occasionally interrupt flow, yet the overall organisation remains clear, meeting the descriptors for the upper band.

The student demonstrates clear knowledge of Japanâs post-war economic history, labour culture and relevant IR theory, but sections occasionally drift into generalisations (e.g., âJapan has been adept to an imperialist system for much of its historyâ) or anecdotal assertions.  Source use is partially effective rather than consistently so, warranting a mid-band 3.

The essay weighs collective versus individual perspectives and briefly evaluates government policies, but evaluation is uneven and sometimes descriptive.  Counter-arguments (e.g., Japanâs relatively low unemployment compared with OECD peers) are mentioned only in passing.  Consequently the discussion is âgoodâ but not âfocused and criticalâ enough for the higher bands.

Essential layout elements are present: title page with word count, consistent font, labelled tables/figures and Harvard-style bibliography.  Minor issues (missing page numbers in the supplied extract, inconsistent superscript formatting) prevent full marks but the majority of requirements are met.

Technical terms such as karÅshi, zanryÅ, neoliberalism, hegemony and critical theory are mostly used accurately, though syntax errors and occasional mistranslations (âadept to an imperialist systemâ) detract from full consistency.  This supports a score of 3 within the 3-4 band.

The topic â the effect of the 2007-09 recession on Japanese mental-health indicators â is stated in the title, repeated in the opening sentence and expanded across the introduction.  The student clearly defines the three indicators (depression, anxiety, suicide) and sets them within wider debates on karÅshi, neoliberalism and collective culture.  Explanatory background is rich and directly geared toward showing why the investigation matters, demonstrating comprehensive communication that satisfies the upper-band descriptors.

A broad range of primary statistics (MHLW reports, National Police Agency data) and secondary literature (Han, IMF, HGPI, academic papers) is used, indicating informed source selection.  Nevertheless, the methodology is implicit rather than explicitly outlined; no explanation of criteria for source credibility or data treatment is provided, and some graphs lack provenance.  This places the work solidly in the âmostly completeâ 3-4 band, at the upper end.

The research question is quoted verbatim, is concise, and limits the scope to measurable variables (depression, anxiety, suicide) over a defined time period.  Throughout the essay almost every section signposts its relation to that question, showing a strong connection.  Minor breadth creep into fertility and education widens the focus slightly, so a 5 (not a full 6) is most representative.

Statistical tables, government reports and scholarly articles are integrated into the narrative and underpin most claims; figures on suicide peaks and unemployment are correctly linked to the crisis.  A few citations are incomplete or rely on journalistic sources, and some images lack clear labels, preventing the precision expected of the top band.

annotations.comment: Clear and concise definitions of key Japanese terms (KarÅshi, ZanryÅ) demonstrate strong consideration of audience needs.
The introduction effectively situates the crisis in global and Japanese contexts, presenting a comprehensive rationale for the study.
Integrating Marxist and neoliberal perspectives provides a solid theoretical foundation to examine cultural and economic drivers.
The explanation of Gramsciâs hegemony is well-contextualized for Japan, demonstrating accurate use of IR concepts.
Paragraphs are very dense and lengthy here; consider breaking them into shorter units to enhance readability.
The unsupported claim about imperialist adaptation lacks citation; provide evidence or revise for clarity.
The connection between cultural coercion and labour practices is clearly articulated, reinforcing the argumentâs coherence.
The correlation between unemployment and suicide is asserted but lacks statistical analysis; include regression or correlation coefficients.
The title clearly communicates the focus on Japanâs experience during the global economic crisis, providing a strong contextual entry point for the reader.
The research question is concise and sharply focused on measurable indicators over a defined period, setting up a clear investigative scope.
The table of contents is present but several entries are misnumbered and do not align with section headings in the text.
Including a structured table of contents supports reader orientation and demonstrates thoughtful organization of the investigation.
Firstâperson reflections (âI was unawareâ) can detract from academic tone; rephrase to maintain objective scholarly style.
The discussion of KarÅshi roots in culture and ideology is insightful but would benefit from precise academic references.
The methodology for selecting theoretical frameworks is implied rather than explicit; clarify criteria for inclusion and relevance to the RQ.
Background information outlines global recession drivers but lacks clear linkage to Japanâs unique economic structure.
The chronological overview of crisis causes helps orient readers to macro factors influencing Japanâs economy.
The historical description of Japanâs capitalist transition is detailed but its direct impact on mental health is not sufficiently developed.
Figures lack source labels and axes titles; every graph should include clear provenance and unit designations.
The overview of Japanâs wartime and postwar challenges is informative, but the link to current mental health needs explicit elaboration.
Incorporating IMF and Abenomics context adds depth to background, illustrating economic measures affecting social structures.
The list of abbreviations is useful, but superscript formatting is inconsistent, which may confuse readers.
Page numbers arenât visible; include clear pagination on each page to support navigation and formal presentation requirements.
Citation â(Macrotrends, 2021)â in-text is incomplete; ensure full bibliographic details are present and consistent.
